stories.headline: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes meeting with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: The leaders of the global superpowers are expected to discuss the war in Iran, tariffs, AI and Taiwan during the two-day visit.

stories.headline: Trump Arrives in Beijing to Begin High-Stakes Summit With Xi

stories.headline: Russia launches mass attack against Ukraine with hundreds of drones, Kyiv says

stories.summary: A series of Israeli  strikes on vehicles in Lebanon killed at least 12 people, including a woman and her two children.

stories.headline: At least eight killed in Israeli drone strikes on Lebanon highway
stories.summary: Two children among those killed after three strikes hit cars on the highway in Jiyeh, south of Beirut.

stories.headline: Nvidia C.E.O. Jensen Huang Hitches Ride With Trump to China After Last-Minute Invite
stories.summary: Jensen Huang boarded Air Force One in Alaska, joining a delegation of more than a dozen business leaders accompanying President Trump on his trip to Beijing.

stories.headline: Hamas 'systematically' used sexual violence in 7 October attacks, report finds
stories.summary: An Israeli commission's two-year investigation concludes Hamas systematically used sexual violence on 7 October and against hostages it held in Gaza, laying groundwork for prosecution.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Reshuffles Investment Bank Leadership
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase named Dorothee Blessing, Kevin Foley and Jared Kaye as co-heads of its global investment banking business. Katherine Doherty has more on the shakeup on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Lone Star-Owned German Bank IKB Is Exposed to Collapse of UK Mortgage Firm MFS
stories.summary: A German bank owned by US investment firm Lone Star Funds is among the growing list of lenders exposed to Market Financial Solutions Ltd., the latest example of how the local UK firm’s collapse has spilled across foreign credit markets.

stories.headline: Nvidia's CEO Is a Late Add to Trump's China Trip
stories.summary: President Donald Trump invited Nvidia co-founder Jensen Huang to join him on his trip to China at the last minute. This brings AI and chips into the spotlight. Tim Cook and Elon Musk also went along. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern is traveling with the president and reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nvidia Chips, Boeing Jets: Stock Traders Focus on Trump in China

stories.headline: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
stories.summary: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.

stories.headline: Chinese court awards compensation to sacked worker replaced by AI

stories.headline: Could South Africa’s Ramaphosa be impeached over ‘cash-in-sofa’ scandal?
stories.summary: South African president came to power on anticorruption mandate - and could go now for stuffing cash in furniture.

stories.headline: Oil stocks drain at record pace as IEA warns of renewed price swings
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency says the oil market is likely to remain in deficit until the final quarter of the year, as disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz continue to constrain supply, increasing the risk of renewed price volatility.

stories.headline: Is there a hantavirus vaccine? Scientists race to develop one for new strain

stories.summary: Brussels is considering using revenues from the EU’s carbon market, the Emissions Trading System to cushion farmers from soaring energy and fertiliser prices, according to a document seen by Euronews.

stories.headline: Ghana to evacuate 300 from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests

stories.headline: Polymarket Trading Volume Declines for First Time Since August
stories.summary: Trading volume on Polymarket has declined for the first time in eight months, a setback that comes as its founder acknowledges missteps and its chief rival Kalshi Inc. continues to grow.

stories.headline: BlackRock Expects Increased Downgrades for State Borrowers
stories.summary: The market for state and local government debt is becoming “less forgiving,” according to BlackRock Inc.’s Pat Haskell, who leads the firm’s municipal bond group.

stories.headline: PE-Owned Insurers Boosting Private Credit Holdings, Study Finds
stories.summary: Life insurance companies owned by private equity firms have quietly reshaped their portfolios, piling into higher-yielding alternative credit in a shift that’s entangled the industry with the broader financial system, according to a study from researchers at the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago.

stories.headline: Maldives jails two journalists for reporting on president’s alleged affair

stories.headline: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report

stories.headline: Family Offices Build Stake in Miner After Stock Soars 900%
stories.summary: For decades, the risky world of early-stage mining ventures was the playground of institutional funds and commodity trading houses. But after a more than 900% rally in a little-known Canadian potash developer last year, a new class of investors is moving in: family offices.

stories.headline: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on ‘life support,’ calls latest proposal ‘garbage’

stories.headline: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants

stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.
